name: hwm_anchored_stop_distance_widens_stop_moves_down
description: AC-3 (H-1): the trailing stop is HWM-anchored. Two consecutive ticks at the SAME high-water mark but with a WIDER vol-scaled distance on tick 2 (mid-day vol spike). The resolved stop_trigger_level MUST move DOWN tick-to-tick — this is the whole point of the fix. The pre-fix code froze the resolved level via the previously_persisted_stop_level max-clamp and would NOT let it drop.
derivation: Both ticks: symphony_vol=1.0 -> dynamic_activation=max(0.4,min(3.0,1.0))=1.0; threshold=1.0-0.2=0.8. current_return on both ticks (0.5) < 0.8 so hwm_hold_ticks stays 0 and breakeven never locks. Stop = base_stop_level (not locked, no floor). Tick 1: base_stop_level = HWM(3.0) - distance(1.0) = 2.0. Tick 2: base_stop_level = HWM(3.0) - distance(2.5) = 0.5. The HWM is identical (3.0) on both ticks; only the vol-scaled distance widened (1.0 -> 2.5). Expected: stop drops 2.0 -> 0.5. A pre-fix impl threading previously_persisted_stop_level=2.0 into tick 2 would clamp to max(2.0, 0.5)=2.0 and FAIL this fixture.
invariant: stop_trigger_level[tick2] < stop_trigger_level[tick1]; the resolved level is NOT ratcheted, it tracks HWM - distance each tick
